title: Learning the Transition Model B

runtime.expected_outcome: Over episodes, per-action B-matrix columns concentrate on the observed (s_{t-1}, a_t -> s_t) transitions, even when the initial B was diffuse.

symbols.b^(a): Dirichlet counts for the action-conditioned transition matrix

audiences.kid: After enough walks, you learn which door leads where. The agent builds its own map.
audiences.real: DirichletUpdateB updates per-action transition counts from the agent's belief chain. Over episodes B converges to the real transition dynamics.
audiences.equation: b^(a)_{j,k} += q(s_t=j) q(s_{t-1}=k) q(a_{t-1}=a). Normalise columns to recover B^(a).
audiences.derivation: Per-action Dirichlet-categorical update under mean-field factorisation. Converges at rate 1/n under standard mixing assumptions (Friston et al. 2016, App B).

orchestrate.objective: Train the B matrix across episodes so the agent learns its own dynamics model.
orchestrate.role: Transition-learning tutor paralleling the A-matrix recipe.
orchestrate.context: corridor_turns, learning_enabled=true, start with a uniform per-action B and watch it concentrate.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 7.
